_README: THIS FILE IS AN EXAMPLE. IT IS NOT LOADED. IT IS NAMED mcp.example.json ON PURPOSE.
Rename it to .mcp.json ONLY if you know you want these, and only after editing every path
below to point at software that exists on YOUR machine. Shipping it live would break on
every computer but the author's.
WHAT THIS FILE CAN AND CANNOT DO — the honest version:
CAN: declare how to launch a local MCP server that is ALREADY INSTALLED on your machine.
CANNOT: install that server. Cannot ship another vendor's desktop app. Cannot ship a data
corpus. Cannot install a Desktop Extension (.mcpb) — that is a SEPARATE CHANNEL,
enforced: admin controls can disable .mcpb installs while leaving plugins untouched.
SO: Anthropic's STOCK iMessage extension and Apple Notes are Desktop Extensions — a plugin
cannot install them, and neither can this file. Install those at Settings > Extensions > Browse.
BUT this package SHIPS its own fixed iMessage reader in companions/imessage-fixed/, and that one
is a plain Python (standard-library) server that CAN run as a raw local MCP server — see the
_imessage_fixed_reader example below. Either route needs Full Disk Access, which only you grant.
THE HONEST COST: every local server here ties your brief to THIS machine — a scheduled brief
that depends on it runs on a waking Mac, not the cloud, so it fires on wake instead of on time.
(The vendor's own docs disagree on the exact mechanism; see references/VOLATILE.md, Scheduling.)
Enable a rung here when its capability is worth that trade: name the gain, accept the cost, turn
it on. That is the whole decision.
See references/STACK.md and assets/where-it-runs.mermaid.
NEVER PUT A SECRET IN THIS FILE. Anyone on the machine can read it. Use ${ENV_VAR}.
THE NAMED MENU is references/DEPENDENCIES.md — every real connector (Todoist, Gmail, Bee,
Reflect, Hevy, health, notes, Mac automation), which are required, and the exact path to turn
each on. This file only shows the SHAPES a local server takes; that file tells you what to pick.

_imessage_fixed_reader._what: The package's own read-only iMessage reader (companions/imessage-fixed/) run as a raw local MCP server. Fills ~~inbound[] and ~~evidence[] slots — a text that moved a plan.
_imessage_fixed_reader._alt: ALTERNATIVE to the .dxt install: instead of double-clicking imessage-fixed.dxt, point a raw MCP config at server/main.py. Same code either way. Needs Full Disk Access, which only you can grant.
_imessage_fixed_reader._honest: Local: the Messages database lives on your Mac, so a scheduled brief that reads it runs on a waking Mac, not the cloud. Python standard-library only, no network, no writes.

_lifelog._what: A wearable that records your conversations. Fills an ~~evidence[] slot.
_lifelog._honest: There is no drop-in replacement and the blind spot is narrow. It records everyone around you, most of whom did not consent, so it is a deliberate choice rather than a default one — enable it if the corroboration is worth that cost to the people in the room.
_lifelog._install: Vendor's own installer, then its CLI. This plugin cannot install it.
_lifelog._why_example: The paths below are examples. A venv path is not a library — it is one person's data corpus. The leading underscore documents; it does not disable — renaming this file to .mcp.json would make every entry here live.

_notes_local_server._what: Some notes apps (e.g. Reflect) run a local HTTP server; the connector talks to that. Fills an ~~intent[] slot.
_notes_local_server._honest: The dependency is THE APP RUNNING ON YOUR MACHINE. A plugin cannot ship another vendor's desktop app. If the app is closed, this is dead.
_notes_local_server._substitute: Notion (hosted, no local server), Apple Notes (extension), a folder of Markdown, or mail-to-yourself — which needs no connector at all because the mail role already covers it.

_npx_published_package._what: The most common shape for a community connector: a published npm package launched with npx. Covers fitness logs (e.g. Hevy), a bridge to a notes app's local endpoint (mcp-remote), and most third-party MCPs. Fills whatever role the tool serves — often ~~evidence[].
_npx_published_package._honest: Runs locally, so a brief that depends on it pins to a waking Mac. The package is public; your API key is not — keep it in env, never in this file.
_npx_published_package._install: Nothing to install ahead of time — npx fetches the package on first run. You still need the account and key from the vendor.

_python_bridge._what: Shape only — a local python MCP server run from a virtualenv.
_python_bridge._honest: Anthropic recommends against bundling Python: compiled dependencies do not port. Only Node ships with Claude Desktop.

hosted_connectors: Gmail, Google Calendar, Todoist, Notion, Slack, Linear and the rest are NOT declared here.
They are not local servers — you add them once in Claude's own connector settings, they work
on every surface including mobile, and they keep your brief cloud-eligible. That is strictly
better than anything this file can do.
Each one still sets its own plan/admin gate on top: Slack is paid-plans-only and also needs a
Slack admin; Microsoft 365 needs a work tenant. Setup walks you through them, and
references/VOLATILE.md carries the current gates and click paths, dated and sourced.
